meta.title: The Patriot Act and the Internet: Recent Developments
meta.description: Passed in the aftermath of the 9/11 attacks on the United States, the USA PATRIOT Act gave the agencies of the federal government greater flexibility to counter terrorism, including on the Internet. Many of the federal government's powers related to the Internet are rooted in the Patriot Act. In 2011, President Obama extended parts of the Patriot Act. Hudson's Center for the Economics of the Internet will host David B. Rivkin, Jr., one of the leading authorities on the Patriot Act, to discuss re
